title: bonia tonkinensis
intro: Bonia tonkinensis, also known as the Tonkin bonia, is a type of plant species in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Bonia tonkinensis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
